name: Felafel Balls
recipeInstructions:
1. Cover beans with water and soak overnight.
2. Drain beans, rinse, and put in a large pot. Add fresh water and 2 teaspoons salt, then bring to a boil. Reduce heat and simmer for 1 1/2 hours. Remove from heat and drain.
3. Soak cracked wheat in water for about 20 minutes. Soak bread separately.
4. Grind garbanzo beans with garlic and parsley until no visible liquid remains. Squeeze out excess moisture.
5. In a large bowl, combine ground mixture with lemon juice, remaining salt, pepper, and eggs. Mix lightly.
6. Add drained cracked wheat and half of the bread crumbs to the mixture. Stir well and mix thoroughly.
7. Gradually add more bread crumbs until the mixture is firm enough to shape felafel balls (about 1 inch in diameter). If too soft, add more breadcrumbs until the desired consistency is reached. Cover with waxed paper and refrigerate for several hours.
8. Heat oil in a heavy skillet to 375 degrees F. Fry felafel balls in batches on both sides until deep golden brown. Remove with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels. Serve immediately.
